JtIrl MINISTRY OF POWER NOTTFICATION New Delhi, the l Tth August,2006 G.s.R 481(E).-In exercise of the powers confened by sub-section (l) of sectionl62-and clause (z) of sub-section (2) of section 176 of the Electicity Act, 2003, (36 of 2003), the Central Govemment hereby makes the following rules for Qualihcations, Powers and Functions of Chief Etectrical lnspedor and Elecbical Lnspectors. l. Short title and commencement, - (l) These rules may be called the Qualifications, Powers and Functions of Chief ElecAical Inspector and Electrical Inspectors Rules, 2006. ' (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. Definitions.- (1) In thcsc itles, uiiicss iirc courcxl orherwise.requires,- (a) "Act" means the Elecfticity Act,2003; (b) "Appellate Authority" in respect ofan appeal against the order of an Electrical lrspector, means the Chief Electrical Inspector, and in respect of an appeal against the order of the Chief Electrical Inspector, means the Cenhal Govemment. (c) "Section" meaas section of the Act. (d) "lnspectot'' means a Chief Electrical Inspector or Electrical Inspector as the case may be. ' (2) Words and expression used and not defined in these rules but defined in the Electricity Act, 2003 (36 of 2003), shall have tle meanings respectively assigned to them in that Act. 3 Applicability of Rules - These rules shall apply in respect of following: 1 (D a generating company wholly or pa*ly owned by the Central Govemment; any inter-State generation, transmission, trading or supply of el ectricity and with respect to any mines, oil-fields, railways, (ii) 1 -- 6 of 10 -- -{iin qx {qY 7 r qrr- q I{-i 1 national highways, airports, telegraphs, broadcasting stations and any works of defence, dockyard, nuclear power installations; (iii) National Load Despatch Centre and Despatch Centre; and Regional Load (iv) any works or electric installation belonging to the Central Government or under its control. 4. Qualification for Chief Electrical Inspector.- No person shall be appointed to be a Chi ef Electrical Inspector unless- (a) he possesses a degree in electical engineering or its equivalent from a recognized University or Institution; and (b) he ha-s been regularly engaged for a period ofat least twenty years in the practice of electrical engineering of which not less than two years horrc haen cncnl in an elent#^al n' 'no^ha-i^rl c-^ine-*i-^ 'r,n-t:"h^- w,r6Liler '116 rr vr r\.r,r\,P or in qeneration or transmission or distrihrrtion of elecfricitv, or i!! the administration of the Act and rules thereunder, in a position of responsibility. 5. Qualifibations for Electrical Inspectors.- (l) No person shall be appciinted'to be an Electrical Ingpector unless - (a) he possesses a degree in Electrical Engineering or its equivalent from a recognized University or Institution; and (b) he has been regularly engaged for a period ofat least Gn years in the practice of elec,trical engineerihg, of ivhich not less than one year has been spent in an elechical or rdechanical engineering workshop or in generation or transmission or dishibution of electricity, or in the administration of the Act and rules thereunder, in a position of responsibility. (2) The person appointed as Electricsl lnspector shall undego such treining as the Central Govemment. may consider it necessary for the pupose and such trainiog shall be completed to the satisfaction of the Govemment. -- 7 of 10 -- I TI{E CA-GTTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY lPaxrII-Sec.l(i)l 7 . Powers of Entry and inspection. - For carrying out inspections as refered to in rule 6 above, - (l) The Inspector may,enter, inspect and examine any place, carriage or vessel in which he has reason to believe that there is any appliance or apparatus used in the generation, tmnsmission, transformation, conversion, <lrstribrfion or use of energy and may carry out tests ther€in, (2) Every supplier, consumer, owner and occupier shall afford all reasonable facilities to any sueh Inspector to make such €xaminations and 'tests as may be necessary to satisry himself al to the due observance of the safety regulations as specified by the Authority under sectior 53 ofthe Act. i hc iDolan !,ioctnctry Kules, rrfo maoe unoer sccuon J / oI tne lnolan El-^+;^:h, A^. l()rn /.-^,., -^-^^t^l\ ^L^!t ^--.:-'.- +- L^:- f---- rltl ^l-- Li=CUiUiiJ i\;4, a> 7n \rivii i!lJi.;qi\/\r., orr..rr \.{.,rrurruv lu uE rlr rt r99 llll tlrl. regulations under section 53 ofthe Act are made. (3) An Inspector may require a supplier of the electricity to sublnit to him a list of all persors supplied with enerry by him, the addresses at which zuch enegr is supplied, the month of connecting services, the voltage of supply, the connected load and the purpose of supply and the supplier shall comply with such requisitions. (4) Every licensee and every owner of a generating station shall, if required so to do by an Inspector, provide reasonable means fo carrying out all tests, specified under the Act or regulations thereunder, of the applianccs or appaxafi$ used for the supply or use of ene.rry by him, as the case may be. (5) Upon such inspection, an Inspector may serve an order, within 15 days fiom the date of such inspection, in the Form A, to any licensee, consum€r, owner or occupier, calling upon him to comply with any specified regulation and the person so served shall thereupon comply with the order within the period specified therein, ard shall repoft in writing to the Insp€ctor serving the order mentioning therein as to when the order has beslt complied with: 6. Powerg oftte ChtefElectrical Inspector and Electrical Inrpector - The ChicfElecEical Inspector and the Elecnical Inspector shall have powers to inspect the wor*s and electricel installations in his area in respcct of whidr, such ao lnspwtor has been directcd by the Cenral Govefiunent to €r(erpise his powers and perform frmctions under sub-sectiqr (l) of the Section 162 of the Act. I -- 8 of 10 -- IqrqI-r1r'B.rti)j qlfd 4I {nFLl : 3I{[]I]I'I Provided that, if within the period specified in the aforesaid order an appeal is tiled against the order by the person on whom such order has been sen'ed, the appellate authority may suspend its operation pending the decision of the appeal. 8. Appeals. - (1) An appeal against an order served under these rules shall lie - (2) In the case of an order of Chief Electrical Inspector on an appeal preferred to him under clause (a) of suFrule ( I ), a further appeal shall lie to the Central Government. (3) Every appeal made under sub.rule.(l) shall be in writing and shall be accompanied by a copy ofthe order appealed against and shall be presented within three months of the date on which such order has been served or delivered, as the case may be. (4) An appeal shall be disposed off within ninety days from the date of receipt of the appeal. FORMA FORM OF ORDER 'ro l. Whereas the installation was inspected on..,..and whereas it appears to me that you have not complied with the rules made under sub-section (l) of section 162 and the regulations made under section 53 of the Act, in the following respect (particulars to be given where necessary) namely ......you are hereby called upon to comply with the said rule(s)/regulations(s) on or betbre . , .. . . . . .. day of ,......and to report compliance in writing to me. 2. An appeal may be filed against this order under rule 8 of the aforesaid Rules, u,ithin three months of the date on which this order is served or ,sLo. q7lb6-7 (a) if the order is served by an Electrical Inspector, to the Chief Electrical Inspector; (b) if the order is served by a Chief Etectrical Inspector, to the Central Govemment. -- 9 of 10 -- I THE CAZETTE oF INDIA L\TR.\ORD]\ ARl- delivered but this order must be cornplied with, notwithstanding such appeal, unless the appellate authority on or t,etbre the date specified in paragraph above. suspends its operation.
